notes: Runs perfectly right up until the ending. Goes to white and freezes.
title: The Room Three

notes: The game runs flawlessly until near the end. There's a cinematic sequence where the screen suddenly turns white and freezes.
title: The Room Three

notes:
As said before the game run perfect until the ending cinematic. Because of that the game can't be finished and the achievements can't be unlocked. The problem also existed for win 7 users, and came from the fact that win 7 use its own version of video codecs (window's Media Player or wmp) compared to win 8 and 10. 

The game is now compatible with wmp codecs, but the wmp version proposed by winetricks is outdated. Its safe to assume that the problem come from here.
title: The Room Three

notes: Everything seems to work after adding "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" to launch options. One minor graphics glitch required me to turn down graphics details temporarily.
title: The Room Three

notes: It works properly until the end. In the final video hangs on a white screen. The problem seems to be due to outdated Codec.
title: The Room Three

title: The Room Three

notes: Game fails to launch, 'Failed to initialize graphics'. Launch Option, PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%, works for The Room One & Two - but not for this game.
title: The Room Three

notes: Hangs at the ending cinematic, as mentioned by others.  The game has a sort of New Game+ mode with alternate endings which won't be unlocked because of this.  You also won't get Steam Achievements for completing the game.
title: The Room Three

notes: The game works perfectly until the ending video, which freezes the game and makes you unable to finish the end (and reach any of the four endings)
title: The Room Three

notes: Works well until game ending video tries to play then a white screen is displayed.  Sometimes rotating puzzles seemed to behave chaotically with small mouse movements.
title: The Room Three

notes:
The game works perfectly without any tweaks until the ending cutscenes. The ending cutscenes require some DLLs from Windows 7 (10 does not work) and a few registry edits. 
The changes required can be seen here: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1102#issuecomment-419678627
title: The Room Three

notes: The game works great until the end. The video at the ending does not work so you basically cannot finish the game.
title: The Room Three

notes:
By default, the game set the average graphics settings. I set it to the maximum. At first, everything was not bad, the game showed 30-50 fps. The spread of FPS depended on the size and fullness of locations. For comparison, Windows also has a spread, but there the game set the default high settings, and at the maximum it shows 40-60fps.
But after receiving 2 pyramids, performance dropped dramatically. The game began to show an average of 10-15fps, the more locations opened, the lower it became, which dropped to 8.

From the good news, it turned out that performance spoils only 1 parameter, this is anti-aliasing. If you turn it off, the game will show as in Windows almost stable 60fps. The remaining settings can be left to the maximum.
From the bad news. As previously written, the game cannot be completed due to non-working videos
title: The Room Three
